questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.hint: Think of four quarters totaling a dollar.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.explanation: 81 ÷ 9 = 9. Put the zero back on the end to make 90.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: A pastry shop sells boxes of donuts. If you buy 9 boxes and each box has 4 donuts inside, how many donuts do you have?

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If Eli reads exactly 15 pages of his book every single day for 5 days in a row, how many pages does he read total?

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: Which fraction represents the smallest amount?
questions.hint: The larger the denominator, the smaller the individual sliced pieces are.
questions.explanation: Splitting an item into 5 parts yields smaller pieces than splitting it into 2, 3, or 4 parts.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: Subtract 11/12 - 5/12. Write your answer as a fraction.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: An angle that is larger than 90 degrees but smaller than 180 degrees is called a(n):
questions.hint: This word sounds wide or blunt.
questions.explanation: An obtuse angle is defined as any individual angle measuring between 90 and 180 degrees.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: What is the perimeter of a rectangle with a length of 14 meters and a width of 6 meters?
questions.hint: Add the length and width together, then multiply that sum by 2.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: How many total years are there in 1 century?
questions.hint: Think of how many cents are in a dollar.
questions.explanation: A century is defined precisely as a period of 100 years.
